mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate level reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and lowers the opportunity cost of holding stocks versus cash, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days on an always-on basis.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or low-rate periods show no statistically significant excess return versus high-rate periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes earnings downgrades and risk-off equity positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window following trigger activation.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of a high nominal federal funds rate combined with decelerating CPI growth implies a rising real policy rate that tightens financial conditions faster than inflation compensation, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. Neither series alone captures this effect since nominal rate level and inflation trend must be evaluated jointly.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS level exceeds trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL YoY growth rate by more than 1pp
failure_condition: Rank IC of the real-rate proxy interaction with forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or triggered instances show no significant negative return bias (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and forward consumer confidence, but this effect on equities is materially amplified only during monetary tightening regimes where credit costs constrain construction financing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days specifically in high-rate environments.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% over trailing 3 months while FEDFUNDS > 3%
failure_condition: Negative HOUST trend during high-rate periods fails to produce below-median forward 60d returns, or effect disappears (Rank IC < 0.02) when tested only within tightening-regime subsample.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding real economic output and corporate revenue capacity, producing a modest always-on positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets incrementally price in improving fundamentals.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and forward 5d returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, indicating no exploitable short-horizon signal.

mechanism_narrative: When total nonfarm payroll growth decelerates below a threshold rate of change, it signals an approaching turning point in labor demand that precedes broad market repricing of growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days once the deceleration crosses the threshold.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below +50,000 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive or flat forward 20d returns in more than 50% of cases, or Rank IC stays below 0.03 across any 3-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Resilient retail sales growth occurring simultaneously with a rising unemployment rate indicates consumer spending is being sustained by savings drawdown or credit expansion rather than income growth, an unsustainable divergence that predicts a short-horizon negative reversal in S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets anticipate consumption normalization.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth > 3% while UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: Triggered divergence episodes fail to produce negative median forward 5d returns, or Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of above-trend real GDP growth in a post-shock recovery regime, positive GDPC1 surprises confirm broadening economic expansion and reinforce risk-asset allocation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; this relationship is specific to recovery regimes and does not hold in mature expansions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and forward 60d returns is below 0.03 within recovery-regime subsamples, or the positive relationship also appears equally strong in mature-expansion subsamples, invalidating regime specificity.
